ddls:
CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject */,
  "subject_type" TEXT /* Type of the subject (e.g., Mathematics, Theory) */,
  "class_number" INTEGER /* Class number of the subject */,
  "date_offered" TEXT /* Date when the subject is offered */,
  "subject_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the subject */,
  "credits" INTEGER /* Number of credits the subject is worth */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department offering the subject */,
  "prerequisite_subject_id" TEXT /* ID of a prerequisite subject, if applicable */,
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instructor teaching the subject */,
  "room_number" TEXT /* Room where the subject is offered */,
  "time_slot" TEXT /* Time slot during which the subject is offered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subjects_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subjects_instructor_id FOREIGN KEY ("instructor_id") REFERENCES instructors ("instructor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subjects_prerequisite_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("prerequisite_subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "courses" (
  "course_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each course */,
  "course_name" TEXT /* Name of the course */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the course */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the course in years or semesters */,
  "total_credits" INTEGER /* Total number of credits required to complete the course */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department offering the course */,
  "program_type" TEXT /* Type of program (e.g., Undergraduate, Graduate) */,
  "admission_requirements" TEXT /* Admission requirements for the course */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the course starts */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the course ends */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("course_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_courses_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "course_subjects" (
  "course_id" INTEGER /* ID of the course */,
  "subject_id" TEXT /* ID of the subject */,
  "semester" INTEGER /* Semester in which the subject is offered within the course */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year in which the subject is offered within the course */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("course_id", "subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instructors" (
  "instructor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instructor */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the instructor */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the instructor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the instructor */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the instructor */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the instructor belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instructor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instructors_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Administrator, Teacher, Student) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "enrollments" (
  "enrollment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each enrollment */,
  "student_id" INTEGER /* ID of the student */,
  "course_id" INTEGER /* ID of the course */,
  "enrollment_date" TEXT /* Date of enrollment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the enrollment (e.g., Active, Completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("enrollment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_enrollments_student_id FOREIGN KEY ("student_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_enrollments_course_id FOREIGN KEY ("course_id") REFERENCES courses ("course_id")
)
